subtitle: When the Earth Trembled: Skandagupta’s Stand Against the Huns

panels.action: Skandagupta stands in full Gupta armor on a wind-swept frontier ridge, one hand on his sword, behind him a hazy vision of Huna horsemen and distant pillars rising from the plains.

dialogue.text: In the twilight of the Guptas, Skandagupta rose like a last bright flame, holding back the thunder of the Huns as the golden age dimmed.

i18n.hi: गुप्तों की सांझ में स्कन्दगुप्त आख़िरी लौ बन उठा, सुनहरे युग के ढलते ही हुणों की गड़गड़ाहट थामते हुए।

panels.action: A vast view of the Gangetic plains in their prime: walled cities, monasteries, irrigated fields and caravan roads shining under a soft dawn haze.

dialogue.text: This was the Gupta age in its fullness: learning in stone halls, numbers on palm leaves, poets and astronomers under quiet oil lamps, and fields heavy with grain along the great rivers.

i18n.hi: ये गुप्तों का पूरा खिलना था: पत्थर की पाठशालाएँ, ताड़पत्रों पर अंक, दीयों तले कवि–ज्योतिषी, और महान नदियों के किनारे झुकी फसलें।

panels.action: A younger Skandagupta, in simple royal garments without crown, stands at a palace balcony, looking over Pataliputra’s busy streets, his face thoughtful and alert.

dialogue.text: Born into power yet not born emperor, Skandagupta watched this order with clear eyes, knowing that a king’s son was not always a king, and the empire’s peace was a woven, fragile cloth.

i18n.hi: सत्ता में जन्मा, पर जन्म से सम्राट नहीं, स्कन्दगुप्त ने साफ़ आँखों से देखा कि राजा का बेटा हमेशा राजा नहीं होता, और साम्राज्य की शांति नाज़ुक बुनावट है।

panels.action: Inside a dim royal hall, Skandagupta stands before elders and ministers in tense debate, his hand resting on a sword, while anxious courtiers cluster in shadow.

dialogue.text: When Kumaragupta died, the weave began to fray. Amid whispers of rival claims and the rise of the Pushyamitras, Skandagupta seized the burden, restoring his house by sword and will.

i18n.hi: कुमारगुप्त के जाते ही यह बुनावट उधड़ने लगी। दावों की फुसफुसाहट और पुष्टि-मित्रों के उभार के बीच, स्कन्दगुप्त ने तलवार और जज़्बे से अपना घर फिर खड़ा किया।

panels.action: On a smoky plain, Skandagupta rides at the head of Gupta infantry and elephant corps against rebel forces, banners whipping, dust clouding the horizon.

dialogue.text: First came war within. The Pushyamitras rose in the heartland; Skandagupta met them in harsh fields and broken towns, beating down their challenge to keep the imperial line unbroken.

i18n.hi: पहले भीतर की जंग आई। पुष्टि-मित्र हृदयभूमि में उठे; स्कन्दगुप्त ने सूखे मैदानों व उजड़े नगरों में उनसे टकरा कर राजवंश की कड़ी टूटने नहीं दी।

panels.action: Stonecutters chisel meticulous Gupta Brahmi letters into a shining pillar as an official reads from a scroll; Skandagupta is seen in the background, small but commanding.

dialogue.text: On the pillar at Bhitari, future ages would read his own claim: that he restored the fallen fortunes of the Guptas and took again the lion seat of power.

i18n.hi: भितरी के स्तंभ पर आने वाले युग पढ़ेंगे उसका दावा: उसने गुप्तों का गिरा भाग्य उठाया और फिर से सिंहासन पर बैठा।

panels.action: A bleak northwestern frontier: dry scrub, a winding track, and in the distance a dark wave of mounted figures approaching in loose formation.

dialogue.text: But while he held the centre, the edges shook. From beyond the northwestern passes came newcomers—Alchon Hunas, grim riders from far plains, testing the wealth of this soft, ordered land.

i18n.hi: पर जबकि वह केंद्र थामे था, किनारे डोल उठे। उत्तर–पश्चिमी दर्रों के पार से आए नए लोग—अल्चोन हुण, दूर के मैदानों के सवार, इस सजी–धजी समृद्ध भूमि को टटोलते हुए।

panels.action: A tight shot of Huna horsemen galloping: fur-trimmed tunics, high boots, lamellar armor, some with distinctive skull-like helmets, bows slung, faces hard and wind-burnt.

dialogue.text: Recent learning names them Alchon Huns, not the later Hephthalites of other tales, but their hooves sounded the same: a thunder of hooves, an art of sudden raid, a hunger for tribute.

i18n.hi: आज के विद्वान उन्हें अल्चोन हुण कहते हैं, बाद के हेफ़थलियों से अलग; पर उनके ख़ुरों की गर्जना वही थी: घुड़सवारों की बिजली, अचानक धावे की कला, और लगान की भूख।

panels.action: In a torchlit war council tent, Skandagupta stands over a rough map of the northwest, markers for fortresses and routes; generals watch, tense and weary.

dialogue.text: News came of towns harried, forts probed, merchants robbed in Gandhara’s shadows. Skandagupta turned from civil war to foreign storm, drawing breath once more to defend an empire already exhausted.

i18n.hi: खबर आई कि गांधार की छाँहों में नगर लुटे, दुर्ग टटोले गए, व्यापारी उजड़े। स्कन्दगुप्त ने गृहयुद्ध से मुँह मोड़ पराई आँधी की ओर रुख किया, थका साम्राज्य बचाने को फिर साँस कसी।

panels.action: A close profile of Skandagupta in armor, jaw set, eyes narrowed in grim resolve, the faint reflection of firelight and a battlefield camp in his gaze.

dialogue.speaker: skandagupta
dialogue.text: If we yield the frontier, we yield the heart. We march.

i18n.hi: सीमा छोड़ेंगे तो दिल भी जाएगा। हम कूच करते हैं।

panels.action: A broad battlefield view: on one side ordered Gupta lines with elephants and archers; on the other, swirling Huna cavalry spreading wide wings around them under a lowering sky.

dialogue.text: Somewhere along the northern frontier, in a place the stones did not name, the two worlds met: steel and horsehair, elephants and steppe stallions, calm ranks against circling swarms.

i18n.hi: उत्तरी सीमा पर, जहाँ पत्थरों ने नाम नहीं रखा, दो दुनिया भिड़ीं: इस्पात और घोड़े की अयाल, हाथी और स्तेपी के घोड़े, सधी कतारें और घुमड़ते झुंड।

panels.action: From behind Skandagupta on horseback, sword raised, we see him leading a charge toward the Huna line, his cloak snapping, dust boiling around his mount.

dialogue.text: The Bhitari pillar would later remember the clash simply: that when Skandagupta met the Hunas in combat, ‘it made the earth tremble’. For those who stood there, it was noise, dust, and fear held in check.

i18n.hi: भितरी का स्तंभ बाद में इसे यूँ याद करेगा: जब स्कन्दगुप्त ने हुणों से टक्कर ली, ‘धरती काँप उठी’। वहाँ खड़े सैनिकों के लिए यह ध्वनि, धूल और काबू में पकड़ा डर था।

panels.shot: chaotic mid-shot of melee without gore
panels.action: A frozen instant of battle: Gupta infantry bracing shields, an elephant rearing, Huna riders wheeling back, arrows in the air, faces strained but no wounds visible.

dialogue.text: The Alchon horsemen circled, loosed, and feinted. Gupta lines bent, then locked. Day turned to a long, shaking blur of charges repelled and paths of retreat cut off.

i18n.hi: अल्चोन घुड़सवार चक्कर काटते, बाण छोड़ते, झूठे हमले करते रहे। गुप्त पंक्तियाँ झुकीं, फिर जम गयीं। दिन एक लंबी, काँपती धुंध बना रहा, जहाँ हर धावा टूटा, हर राह काटी गई।

panels.action: Skandagupta, armor scuffed and cloak torn, sits firm in the saddle amid dust, signaling with his sword for his men to press forward as distant Huna riders begin to pull back.

dialogue.text: When the dust thinned, the invaders had yielded the field. Skandagupta held it still, weary yet unbroken, having flung back the first great wave of the Huns at terrible cost.

i18n.hi: जब धूल बैठी, आक्रमणकारी मैदान छोड़ चुके थे। स्कन्दगुप्त अब भी डटा था, थका पर अडिग, हुणों की पहली बड़ी लहर को भारी कीमत पर लौटा चुका था।

panels.action: A twilight scene of the battlefield aftermath: abandoned tents, toppled standards, soldiers resting on their shields; Skandagupta walks among them, head slightly bowed.

dialogue.text: Victory did not mean ease. Behind the praise carved in stone lay the truth: men lost, treasuries drained, allies shaken, an empire saved for a time but left breathing hard.

i18n.hi: विजय का मतलब सुगमता नहीं था। पत्थर की प्रशंसा के पीछे सच्चाई थी: सैनिक खोए, खजाने खाली, मित्र डगमगाए, साम्राज्य कुछ समय को बचा पर हाँफता छोड़ दिया गया।

panels.shot: wide shot of Bhitari pillar in countryside
panels.action: The tall Bhitari pillar rises from the plain, surrounded by simple fields and a few villagers, its polished surface catching the sun.

dialogue.text: Yet the pillar at Bhitari stood as his chosen memory: Skandagupta, who restored his dynasty, broke the Pushyamitras, and hurled back the Hunas, set his name in stone for distant eyes.

i18n.hi: फिर भी भितरी का स्तंभ उसकी चुनी याद बना रहा: स्कन्दगुप्त, जिसने वंश सँभाला, पुष्टि-मित्रों को तोड़ा और हुणों को पछाड़ा, ने अपना नाम दूर की आँखों के लिए पत्थर में बाँध दिया।

panels.shot: establishing wide of Girnar hills and Sudarshana lake
panels.action: A serene view of the Girnar hills near Junagadh, with the Sudarshana Lake glinting below and a stone embankment running along its edge.

dialogue.text: Far away, at the foot of Girnar, another stone tells a quieter tale. There, the old Sudarshana Lake, first tamed in Rudradaman’s day, had burst its embankment in the age of Skandagupta.

i18n.hi: दूर गिरनार की तलहटी में एक और शिला धीमी कथा कहती है। वहाँ पुराना सुधर्शन झील, जिसे पहली बार रुद्रदामन ने बाँधा था, स्कन्दगुप्त के युग में बाँध तोड़ बैठी।

panels.shot: medium shot of officials inspecting broken embankment
panels.action: Parnadatta and his son Chakrapalita stand on a cracked, eroded embankment, looking over a drained lakebed and damaged fields, with workers waiting nearby.

dialogue.text: Under Skandagupta’s name, his governor Parnadatta and Parnadatta’s son Chakrapalita gathered men and means to mend what the waters had broken, even as coins grew scarce and the realm was strained.

i18n.hi: स्कन्दगुप्त के नाम के नीचे, उसके महामात्य पर्णदत्त और पर्णदत्त के पुत्र चक्रपालित ने पुरुष और साधन जुटाकर टूटे बाँध सँवारे, जबकि सिक्के कम थे और राज्य थका हुआ।

panels.action: Workers haul stone blocks into place, some shaping them, others guiding them onto the embankment, while officials oversee the repair against the backdrop of the lake and hills.

dialogue.text: The Junagadh rock inscription records their work: in Gupta Era 138, they repaired the dam, restored the lake, and with it the fields below—proof that the empire’s hands still laboured, though its chest felt tight.

i18n.hi: जूनागढ़ शिला लेख उनका काम गिनता है: गुप्त संवत १३८ में उन्होंने बाँध सुधारा, झील फिर भरी, और साथ ही नीचे के खेत—इस बात का सबूत कि साम्राज्य के हाथ अभी भी काम में थे, भले सीना धकधका रहा था।

panels.action: In a dim treasury chamber, Skandagupta studies a small pile of coins and account scrolls; behind him, shelves are half-empty, and an accountant waits nervously.

dialogue.text: Numismatists, reading his coins, see the mark of these years: issues altered, outputs likely reduced. The emperor who held the line did so with a purse that grew lighter each campaign.

i18n.hi: मुद्रा–विशेषज्ञ उसके सिक्कों में इन वर्षों की छाप देखते हैं: प्रकार बदले, निर्गम शायद घटे। जिसने सीमा थामी, उसने हर अभियान में हलकी होती तिजोरी से ये किया।

panels.action: An older Skandagupta sits in a quiet chamber at night, armor set aside, looking at a rolled inscription and a map; an oil lamp burns low nearby.

dialogue.speaker: skandagupta
dialogue.text: I have bought time with steel and fatigue. May they use it well.

i18n.hi: मैंने समय खरीदा है इस्पात और थकान से। वे इसे सही तरह बरतें।

panels.action: A montage-like scene: the empire’s map subtly cracking at its edges, while in the distance ghostly figures of later Huna kings Toramana and Mihirakula loom over ruined fortresses.

dialogue.text: After Skandagupta’s death, the cracks widened. By the early sixth century, rulers like Toramana and Mihirakula led Huna forces deeper, and the once-great Gupta fabric tore into smaller, worn pieces.

i18n.hi: स्कन्दगुप्त के बाद दरारें चौड़ी हो गईं। छठी सदी की शुरुआत तक तोरामण और मिहिरकुल जैसे शासक हुण सेना लेकर भीतर घुसे, और कभी–महान गुप्त वस्त्र टुकड़ों में बिखर गया।

panels.action: Centuries later, a scholar in simple robes sits cross-legged, writing numerals on a palm-leaf manuscript, with an abacus-like device and an old inscription rubbing nearby.

dialogue.text: Yet the age did not vanish. Its gifts outlived its crowns: the numerals that would travel the world, the texts of law and poetry, the images in stone and bronze that carried memory beyond the fall of walls.

i18n.hi: पर युग मिटा नहीं। उसकी देन ताजों से आगे चली: वे अंक जो दुनिया घूमे, कानून और काव्य के ग्रंथ, पत्थर और कांसे की मूर्तियाँ जो गिरती दीवारों से भी आगे स्मृति ढोती रहीं।

panels.action: A statue-like vision of Skandagupta in armor, half in shadow, half in light, stands against a backdrop of faint Gupta temples and universities fading into darkness at the edges.

dialogue.text: Skandagupta’s triumph over the Hunas was not a new dawn, but a held sunset. His greatness lay in delaying the night so that the light of his age could pass on into other hands, other times.

i18n.hi: स्कन्दगुप्त की हुणों पर जीत नया सबेरा नहीं, थामा हुआ सूरज–डूब था। उसकी महत्ता इस बात में थी कि उसने रात टाल दी, ताकि अपने युग की रोशनी दूसरी हथेलियों और आने वाले समयों तक पहुँच सके।

subtitle_i18n.en: When the Earth Trembled: Skandagupta’s Stand Against the Huns
subtitle_i18n.hi: जब धरती काँपी: स्कन्दगुप्त बनाम हुण
